channel.title: Lumen AI User Guide on Lumen AI — User Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Lumen AI User Guide on Lumen AI — User Guide

item.title: Clinical Scribe
item.description:
You dictated some notes between patients. Now you need to turn them into a proper clinical document — with the right ICD-10 codes, CPT codes, and medication details. The Clinical Scribe agent does all of that for you.

Give It Your Notes

Paste your rough notes, a dictation transcript, or even bullet points. For example:

“55yo male, HTN follow-up. BP 168/96, up from last visit. On lisinopril 20mg. Adding amlodipine 5mg. A1c 6.8 last month, discussing lifestyle changes. ECG normal sinus. Follow up 2 weeks.”

item.title: Finding Information
item.description:
When you ask Lumen a question, it searches for the best available evidence before answering. You can choose where it looks using three simple buttons at the bottom of the chat.

The Three Knowledge Sources

⚡
Clinical Database
Lumen's built-in clinical knowledge base. Contains peer-reviewed guidelines, drug databases, lab reference ranges, and clinical protocols. This is your primary source of truth.
"What are the current USPSTF screening recommendations for lung cancer?"
On by Default
📁
My Documents
Your personally uploaded files — PDFs, notes, research papers, and reference materials. Toggle this on when you want Lumen to search your own library.
"Based on the uploaded protocol, what are the inclusion criteria for this trial?"
🌐
Web Search
Live internet search for the most current information. Useful for breaking research, conference proceedings, or topics not yet in the clinical database.
"What were the key findings from the latest ASCO 2026 presentations on NSCLC?"

How Search Works

When you send a message with search enabled, here’s what happens:

item.title: The Visual Editor
item.description:
The workflow editor is a visual canvas where you build automations by placing tools and connecting them. Think of it like drawing a flowchart — except each box actually does something.

The Canvas

When you create a new workflow, you get a large open canvas. You can:

  • Drag to pan — Move around the canvas
  • Add nodes — Click the palette on the right to browse tools, or right-click the canvas
  • Connect nodes — Drag from an output port (right side of a node) to an input port (left side of another)
  • Click a node — Configure its settings (e.g., what text to input, how many results to return)
  • Delete connections — Click on a connecting line to remove it

Adding a Node

1

Open the tool palette

Click the palette icon on the right side of the editor. You’ll see all available tools organized by category — Inputs, Text Analysis, Medications, Coding, Protocols, and more.
2

Pick a tool

Click the tool you want. It appears on the canvas as a node — a small card with the tool’s name and icon.
3

Position it

Drag the node to where you want it on the canvas. Nodes further to the right generally run later in the workflow.
4

Configure it

Click the node to open its settings. Each tool has different options:

item.title: Typing & Sending
item.description:
The bottom of the screen is where you type. It looks and works like any messaging app — but it has a few extra buttons that let you control where Lumen looks for answers.

The Input Bar

At the bottom of every chat is the input bar. Here’s what each element does:

Clinical Database
My Docs
Web
Message Lumen…
↑

Knowledge Source Toggles

Below the text field are three toggles that control where Lumen searches for information:

item.title: Your First Chat
item.description:
Using Lumen is like texting a very knowledgeable colleague. You ask a question in plain English, and Lumen gives you a clear answer backed by evidence. No menus, no dropdowns, no learning curve.

What You’ll See When You Open Lumen

The screen is simple: a text box at the bottom, and an open space above where the conversation will appear.

✦
New Chat
Message Lumen…
↑

That’s it. The glowing orb means Lumen is ready and waiting for your question.

item.title: Building Blocks
item.description:
Every node in a workflow is a tool that does one thing. Here are all the tools available, grouped by the kind of work they do.

Where Data Comes In

These nodes start your workflow — they’re how you get information into the pipeline.

📝
Text Input
Type or paste text. This is the most common starting point — paste a clinical note, a question, or any text you want to process.
📄
Document Upload
Upload a PDF, Word doc, or text file. The workflow reads and extracts the text automatically.
🎤
Voice Input
Use a dictation transcript as input. Pairs well with Lumen's dictation feature.
🌐
Web Scrape
Fetch and parse content from a URL. Useful for pulling in guidelines or reference material from the web.
Pro

Text Analysis

These nodes read text and extract useful structure from it.

item.title: Check a Drug Interaction
item.description:
You’re rounding on a patient and want to add a new medication. Before you order, you want to make sure it’s safe with what they’re already taking. Here’s how to do that in Lumen.

What to Type

Just describe the situation naturally. Any of these will work:

  • “Are there interactions between warfarin and amiodarone?”
  • “Can I give ketorolac if the patient is on apixaban?”
  • “Check interactions for metformin, lisinopril, and atorvastatin”

You don’t need to use a specific format. Ask it the way you’d ask a pharmacist.

item.title: Diagnostic Support
item.description:
A patient presents with a complex set of symptoms. You need the full picture — triage level, differential, codes, lab interpretation, medication review, and protocols. The Diagnostic Support agent runs all of that from a single prompt.

Give It the Clinical Picture

“62yo male, sudden crushing substernal chest pain radiating to left arm, diaphoresis, BP 92/58, HR 110. PMH: HTN, DM2, former smoker. Meds: metformin 1000mg BID, lisinopril 40mg, aspirin 81mg. Troponin pending.”

item.title: Reading Answers
item.description:
Lumen’s answers look like regular text, but they often include extra pieces — like a list of sources, a progress indicator showing what it searched, or a tappable card with a detailed report. Here’s what each part means.

Anatomy of a Response

Every assistant response follows a consistent structure. Here’s what you’ll see, from top to bottom:

✦
Chat
What is the CHA₂DS₂-VASc score for a 72-year-old woman with hypertension and diabetes?
✦
Thought for 3s
Ran medical calculator
CHA₂DS₂-VASc · age=72, sex=female

The CHA₂DS₂-VASc score is 5 for this patient, which indicates a high stroke risk. Anticoagulation is recommended.

item.title: Research & Evidence
item.description:
Need to look something up in PubMed mid-round? Want to check what the FDA label actually says? Just ask. Lumen searches the major medical databases and brings the results right into your conversation.

Available Tools

📚
PubMed Search
Searches the PubMed/MEDLINE database via NLM E-utilities. Finds relevant articles, fetches abstracts, and ranks results by relevance to your question.
"Find recent systematic reviews on SGLT2 inhibitors and heart failure outcomes"
Research
💊
FDA Drug Labels
Queries the openFDA drug labeling API. Returns official FDA-approved label information including indications, dosing, warnings, and contraindications.
"What does the FDA label say about semaglutide contraindications?"
Regulatory
🧪
Clinical Trials Search
Searches ClinicalTrials.gov for active and completed studies. Filter by condition, recruitment status, and trial phase.
"Find active Phase 3 trials for NASH/MASH treatment"
Research

How PubMed Search Works

When Lumen searches PubMed, you’ll see a tool call block showing the progress:

item.title: Find a Billing Code
item.description:
You know the diagnosis. You just need the code. Instead of searching through a code book or billing software, describe it to Lumen and get the answer immediately.

What to Type

Describe the diagnosis or procedure however you normally would:

  • “ICD-10 for acute on chronic systolic heart failure”
  • “CPT code for laparoscopic cholecystectomy”
  • “What’s the code for type 2 diabetes with diabetic nephropathy?”

What You’ll See

✦
Chat
ICD-10 for acute on chronic systolic heart failure
✦
Looked up ICD-10 codes

The primary code is I50.23 — Acute on chronic systolic (congestive) heart failure.

item.title: How Lumen Thinks
item.description:
You wouldn’t trust a colleague who just said “trust me” without explaining their reasoning. Neither should you trust an AI that way. That’s why Lumen shows you how it arrived at its answer — you can expand the thinking block and see the logic step by step.

What Is the Thinking Block?

When Lumen processes a complex question, it first “thinks” through the problem before responding. This appears as a collapsible block at the top of the response.

item.title: Medication Questions
item.description:
These are the questions that come up a dozen times a shift. “Can I give these two together?” “What’s the drip rate?” “Does this need a dose adjustment for their kidneys?” Just type the question and Lumen handles the rest.

Available Tools

💊
Drug Name Lookup (RxNorm)
Resolves drug names via NLM RxNorm. Works with brand names, generics, abbreviations, and even misspellings. Returns the normalized name, RxCUI, ingredients, and drug class.
"Look up the generic name for Jardiance"
Lookup
⚠
Drug Interactions
Checks drug-drug interactions using the FDA interaction database. Returns severity level, mechanism of interaction, and clinical management recommendations.
"Check interactions between warfarin, amiodarone, and metoprolol"
Safety
💧
IV Drip Calculator
Calculates IV infusion rates from weight-based dosing. Computes mL/hr with titration guidance. Supports common drips like vasopressors, insulin, and heparin.
"Calculate norepinephrine drip at 0.1 mcg/kg/min for an 80kg patient"
Calculator
🩸
Renal Dose Adjustment
Computes creatinine clearance (Cockcroft-Gault), determines CKD stage, and looks up FDA-recommended dose adjustments for renal impairment.
"Renal dose check for vancomycin in a 70-year-old male, 85kg, SCr 2.1"
Calculator
📈
FDA Adverse Events
Searches the FDA FAERS (adverse event reporting) database. Aggregates reaction reports by drug, showing frequencies, outcomes, and severity distributions.
"What are the most reported adverse events for methotrexate?"
Safety
🚨
FDA Drug Recalls
Checks the FDA enforcement and recall database. Shows recall classifications, affected lots, and current status.
"Are there any recent recalls for metformin products?"
Safety

Drug Interaction Example

Here’s what a drug interaction check looks like in action:

item.title: Research Advisor
item.description:
You have a clinical question that requires a real evidence review — not just a quick answer, but what the studies show, what the FDA says, and whether any trials are running. The Research Advisor handles the whole thing.

Give It Your Question

“What is the current evidence for SGLT2 inhibitors in heart failure with preserved ejection fraction?”

The 7-Step Review

1
Extract Research Terms
Identifies the key concepts and builds optimized search queries.
📄
Search Strategy
Terms: SGLT2 inhibitors, HFpEF, dapagliflozin, empagliflozin, heart failure preserved ejection fraction
Filters: RCTs, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, 2020–2026
2
Knowledge Base Search
Searches Lumen's indexed clinical content for baseline context.
📚
Clinical Context4 sources
ACC/AHA HF Guidelines · ESC HFpEF Position Paper · SGLT2i Class Review · HFpEF Diagnostic Criteria
3
PubMed LiteraturePubMed
Searches PubMed for peer-reviewed studies, fetches abstracts, ranks by relevance.
📚
PubMed Results12 articles
Solomon SD et al. DELIVER (2022)Dapagliflozin in HFpEF. NEJM. HR 0.82 (0.73–0.92) for worsening HF or CV death.
Anker SD et al. EMPEROR-Preserved (2021)Empagliflozin in HFpEF. NEJM. HR 0.79 (0.69–0.90) for CV death or HF hospitalization.
Vaduganathan M et al. (2022)SGLT2i across the HF spectrum: meta-analysis. Lancet.
4
FDA Drug DataOpenFDA
Checks FDA prescribing information, approved indications, and safety data.
💊
FDA Label Data
Dapagliflozin (Farxiga)✓ FDA-approved for HF (all EFs)
Empagliflozin (Jardiance)✓ FDA-approved for HF (all EFs)
Key warnings:DKA risk, genital infections, volume depletion
5
Clinical TrialsClinicalTrials.gov
Searches for active and recently completed trials relevant to the question.
🧪
Active Trials3 trials
NCT05396573SGLT2i + GLP-1 RA combination in HFpEF (Phase 3, recruiting)
NCT06012839Dapagliflozin in HFpEF with obesity (Phase 4, active)
6
Drug NormalizationRxNorm
Resolves drug names to canonical identifiers for accuracy.
💊
Drug Profile
Class:SGLT2 inhibitor (sodium-glucose co-transporter 2)
Mechanism:Blocks glucose reabsorption in proximal tubule → glycosuria, osmotic diuresis, cardioprotection
✓
Evidence Synthesis
Combines all evidence into a structured review with conclusions and identified gaps.
📋
Literature ReviewFinal Report
Conclusion: Strong evidence (2 large RCTs, meta-analyses) supports SGLT2i use in HFpEF. Both dapagliflozin and empagliflozin FDA-approved. NNT ~21 for HF hospitalization prevention. Active trials exploring combination therapy and subpopulations.
💡 Tip
Attach a paper for context
Upload a research paper in the Sources panel. The agent will use it as context — finding related studies, newer data, or contradicting evidence.

item.title: Running & Results
item.description:
Once you’ve connected your nodes, running a workflow is one click. Here’s what happens and how to read the results.

Running Your Workflow

1

Click Run

Tap the Run button in the toolbar at the top of the editor. Your workflow saves automatically before executing.
2

Watch it execute

Each node lights up as it runs — you’ll see a progress indicator move through the chain from left to right. A run log panel opens at the bottom showing each step’s status in real time.
3

Review per-node results

As each node finishes, its output appears in the results panel. You can see exactly what every step produced — the entities it extracted, the codes it found, the text it generated.
4

Check the final output

The last node in your chain delivers the final result. If it’s a “Send to Chat” node, the output appears in your chat. If it’s a PDF or slides export, the file is ready to download.

The Run Log

The run log shows each node’s execution with:

item.title: Build a Differential
item.description:
You’re working up a patient and want to make sure you’re not missing anything. Tell Lumen the key symptoms, and it generates a ranked differential with the tests that would help distinguish between them.

What to Type

Include as much clinical detail as you have — symptoms, age, sex, relevant history:

  • “Differential for a 28yo female with acute pleuritic chest pain and tachycardia”
  • “45yo male, 2 weeks of fatigue, night sweats, 10lb weight loss, cervical lymphadenopathy”
  • “What could cause acute onset vertigo in a 60yo with hypertension?”

The more detail you give, the better the differential.

item.title: Codes, Labs & Calculators
item.description:
Nobody went to medical school to memorize billing codes. Describe the diagnosis or procedure in your own words and Lumen finds the right code. It also checks lab reference ranges, verifies Medicare coverage, and runs clinical calculators like CHA₂DS₂-VASc and MELD.

Available Tools

🏥
ICD-10 Lookup
Search ICD-10-CM diagnostic codes. Matches clinical descriptions to specific codes with laterality, severity, and sequencing guidance.
"ICD-10 code for acute anterolateral STEMI, initial encounter"
Coding
🧾
CPT Lookup
Find CPT procedure codes. Resolves procedure descriptions to billing codes with intensity levels and component breakdowns.
"CPT code for laparoscopic cholecystectomy"
Coding
📋
HCPCS Lookup
Search HCPCS Level II codes for DME, supplies, and non-physician services. Covers J-codes for drugs, E-codes for equipment, and more.
"HCPCS code for a standard wheelchair"
Coding
✅
CMS Coverage Lookup
Check Medicare coverage determinations — both National (NCDs) and Local (LCDs). Returns coverage criteria and required documentation.
"Does Medicare cover continuous glucose monitoring for type 2 diabetes?"
Insurance
🧪
Lab Reference Ranges
LOINC-mapped laboratory reference ranges. Returns normal intervals adjusted for units, age, and sex, with clinical significance notes for abnormal values.
"What is the normal reference range for TSH?"
Labs
🧮
Medical Calculator
Validated clinical scoring tools — eGFR, CHA₂DS₂-VASc, MELD, CURB-65, Wells criteria, APACHE II, and more. Returns scores with interpretation.
"Calculate MELD-Na score: bilirubin 3.2, INR 1.8, creatinine 1.5, sodium 131"
Calculator

ICD-10 Code Search in Action

✦
Chat
ICD-10 code for type 2 diabetes with diabetic chronic kidney disease stage 3
✦
Looked up ICD-10 codes
query: "type 2 diabetes diabetic chronic kidney disease stage 3"

For T2DM with diabetic CKD stage 3, you'll need two codes:

E11.22 — Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease
N18.3 — Chronic kidney disease, stage 3 (unspecified)

item.title: Insurance & Appeals
item.description:
Prior auths and claim denials consume hours of your week. Lumen has two agents that do the heavy lifting — one for clinicians handling the paperwork, and one that aggressively fights denials on behalf of patients.

Insurance Manager

For clinicians and billing staff. Describe the procedure and diagnosis, and the agent handles the rest.

“Prior auth for MRI lumbar spine. 55yo with 6 weeks of radiculopathy, failed conservative management with PT and NSAIDs. Payer: UnitedHealthcare.”

item.title: Reports & Files
item.description:
Sometimes a text answer isn’t enough. When you ask about drug interactions, Lumen doesn’t just tell you — it creates a detailed report you can tap to open. Ask for a presentation, and it builds one you can download. These attached files and reports are called artifacts, and they show up as tappable cards in the chat.

What Are Artifacts?

An artifact is a structured, interactive piece of content that Lumen creates during a conversation. They appear as clickable cards within the chat.

item.title: Turn It Into an Agent
item.description:
You’ve built a workflow that works well. Now you can turn it into a reusable agent — something you (or others) can run with one click, without opening the editor.

Exporting as an Agent

1

Click 'Export as Agent'

In the workflow editor toolbar, tap Export as Agent. A dialog opens where you’ll name and describe your agent.
2

Give it a name and description

Choose a clear name that describes what the agent does. Write a short description — this is what other people see if you publish it.

item.title: Document Analyzer
item.description:
You received a consult report, a research paper, or a lengthy clinical document. You need the key findings organized, mapped to codes, and backed with evidence. Upload the document and let the agent do the rest.

Upload and Ask

Attach a PDF or paste the text in the Sources panel, then type:

“Analyze this cardiology consult and summarize the key findings and recommendations”

The 7-Step Analysis

1
Summarize Document
Creates a comprehensive executive summary of the entire document.
📄
Executive Summary
68yo male referred for evaluation of new systolic murmur. Echo reveals moderate-severe aortic stenosis (AVA 0.9 cm², mean gradient 38 mmHg). LVEF preserved at 58%. Coronary angiography recommended prior to valve intervention...
2
Extract Entities
Identifies all clinical entities — conditions, medications, procedures, lab values, measurements.
📄
Clinical Entities
Conditions:aortic stenosis (moderate-severe), exertional dyspnea
Measurements:AVA 0.9 cm², mean gradient 38 mmHg, LVEF 58%
Medications:lisinopril, atorvastatin, aspirin
Procedures:echocardiogram, coronary angiography (recommended)
3
Extract Keywords
Pulls the primary clinical topics for literature searching.
📄
Key Topics
aortic stenosis, TAVR, surgical AVR, valve area, gradient, exercise tolerance
4
Knowledge Base Search
Searches for relevant clinical guidelines and context.
📚
Guidelines3 sources
ACC/AHA Valvular Heart Disease Guidelines 2023 · AS Intervention Criteria · TAVR vs SAVR Decision Framework
5
PubMed LiteraturePubMed
Finds supporting or contradicting evidence from peer-reviewed studies.
📚
PubMed Results8 articles
PARTNER 3 (2019)TAVR vs SAVR in low-risk patients. NEJM.
Carabello BA (2023)Timing of intervention in AS. Circulation.
6
Diagnostic CodesICD-10
🏥
ICD-10 Codes
I35.0Nonrheumatic aortic (valve) stenosis
R06.00Dyspnea, unspecified
✓
Structured Analysis Report
Combines everything into an organized report: summary, key findings, ICD-10 codes, supporting evidence, and clinical recommendations.
📋
Analysis ReportFinal Report
Summary · 6 key findings · 2 ICD-10 codes · 8 supporting articles · 3 guideline citations · Recommended next steps: coronary angio, valve clinic referral, STS risk score
💡 Tip
Compare multiple documents
Upload several documents — reports over time, or competing guidelines — and ask the agent to compare them.

item.title: Draft a Clinical Note
item.description:
It’s the end of the day and you have six notes to finish. Instead of starting from a blank template, tell Lumen the key details and get a draft you can review and sign.

What to Type

Describe the visit the way you’d tell a colleague about it:

  • “Write a SOAP note for a 55yo male with uncontrolled HTN, BP 168/96 today, added amlodipine 5mg, follow up 2 weeks”
  • “Discharge summary: 68yo CHF exacerbation, IV diuresis x3 days, improved, discharge on lasix 40mg”
  • “Referral letter to cardiology for a 42yo with new murmur and exertional dyspnea”

What You’ll Get

Lumen creates a structured note in the standard format:

item.title: Protocols & Decision Support
item.description:
When you need to work through a clinical problem systematically — building a differential, choosing an antibiotic, following a protocol — Lumen does the structured work so you can focus on the patient.

Available Tools

☑
Clinical Checklists
Evidence-based protocol checklists for sepsis bundles, DKA management, central line insertion, surgical safety, and more. Interactive — track completion by checking items.
"Give me a sepsis 3-hour bundle checklist"
Protocols
🏥
Triage Scoring
Emergency triage systems — ESI (Emergency Severity Index), MTS (Manchester Triage), CTAS, and START. Scores patient acuity from clinical presentation.
"Triage a 55-year-old with chest pain, diaphoresis, and BP 90/60"
Emergency
🧠
Differential Diagnosis
Generates a ranked differential diagnosis based on symptoms, demographics, vitals, and history. Searches PubMed and maps to ICD-10. Includes distinguishing features and recommended workup.
"Differential for a 30-year-old with acute onset pleuritic chest pain and dyspnea"
Diagnosis
🦠
Antimicrobial Selector
Selects appropriate antibiotic therapy based on IDSA guidelines. Considers infection type, severity, allergies, and pregnancy status. Returns first/second-line regimens with dosing and duration.
"Best antibiotic for community-acquired pneumonia, PCN allergy, outpatient"
Treatment
💥
Allergy Cross-Reactivity
Identifies cross-reactive drugs and safe alternatives for patients with known drug allergies. Maps allergy patterns and suggests de-sensitization options.
"What can I use instead of cephalexin if the patient has a penicillin allergy?"
Safety
📖
Clinical Guidelines
Searches USPSTF and PubMed for practice guidelines (2020+). Returns recommendations with letter grades and evidence summaries.
"Current guidelines for managing newly diagnosed hypertension"
Guidelines
👍
Clinical Recommendations
Generates prioritized, evidence-graded recommendations (Grade A through D). Context-specific with supporting rationale.
"Recommendations for secondary stroke prevention in a patient with PFO"
Evidence
💳
NPI Lookup
Searches the NPI provider registry. Resolves provider name, specialty, taxonomy, and practice location. Useful for referrals and verification.
"Look up cardiologists in Austin, TX"
Directory

Differential Diagnosis in Action

The differential diagnosis tool is one of Lumen’s most powerful clinical features:

item.title: Where It Gets Its Info
item.description:
Lumen always shows its work. After every answer, you’ll see a “Sources” link that tells you exactly which guidelines, studies, or databases the answer came from — and how closely each one matched your question. Think of it as the bibliography at the end of a paper.

The Sources Chip

After a response that draws on evidence, you’ll see a sources chip:

📚 Sources (4)

Click this chip to expand the list of sources inline, or click “View in panel” to open them in the side panel for a detailed view.

item.title: Health Explainer
item.description:
Your patient just got a diagnosis, a lab result, or a medication change. They look confused. The Health Explainer takes complex medical content and translates it into clear, plain language at an 8th grade reading level — something they can actually read at home.

Paste the Medical Content

“Newly diagnosed atrial fibrillation with RVR, CHA2DS2-VASc 4, started on apixaban 5mg BID and metoprolol succinate 50mg daily. Echo: mild LA dilation, EF 55%. Cardioversion not planned.”

item.title: Patient Records & Notes
item.description:
If your practice has EHR integration set up, Lumen can pull a patient’s current medications, recent labs, and diagnoses right into the conversation. It can also draft clinical notes and check scheduling availability.

⚠️
EHR integration requires setup. These tools are only available after configuring your FHIR connection in Settings > EHR Integration. See your administrator for access.

Available Tools

👤
FHIR Patient Data
Queries your EHR via FHIR R4. Pulls current medications, recent labs, active diagnoses, allergies, and encounter history for a specific patient.
"Pull the current medication list for patient ID 12345"
EHR
📅
Scheduling
Queries the booking system for available appointment slots. Filter by provider, specialty, date range, and location.
"Show available cardiology slots next week"
EHR
📝
Clinical Notes
Auto-generates clinical documentation — SOAP notes, H&P notes, discharge summaries, and referral letters. Structured according to standard clinical formats.
"Generate a SOAP note for today's visit — 45yo with uncontrolled HTN"
Documentation

Clinical Note Generation

Lumen can draft several types of clinical documentation:

item.title: Run a Clinical Calculator
item.description:
You need a clinical score — CHA₂DS₂-VASc, MELD, eGFR, Wells criteria — but you don’t want to look up the formula or find a calculator app. Just tell Lumen about the patient and it runs the right calculator automatically.

What to Type

You don’t even need to name the calculator. Just describe what you need:

  • “What’s the stroke risk for a 72-year-old woman with AFib, hypertension, and diabetes?”
  • “MELD score: bilirubin 3.2, INR 1.8, creatinine 1.5, sodium 131”
  • “Calculate eGFR for a 65yo Black male, creatinine 1.4”
  • “Wells score for PE — tachycardia, recent surgery, no hemoptysis”

What You’ll See

✦
Chat
CHA2DS2-VASc for a 72yo woman with HTN and diabetes
✦
Ran medical calculator
CHA₂DS₂-VASc
5 High stroke risk — anticoagulation recommended
Age 65–74: +1 • Age ≥75: +1 • Female: +1 • HTN: +1 • DM: +1

How It Works

  1. Lumen figures out which calculator to use based on your question
  2. It extracts the patient parameters from your message
  3. It shows the score with interpretation — not just a number, but what it means clinically
  4. If information is missing, it asks you for the specific values it needs

Common Calculators

Calculator What It’s For
CHA₂DS₂-VASc Stroke risk in AFib
HAS-BLED Bleeding risk on anticoagulation
MELD / MELD-Na Liver disease severity
eGFR (CKD-EPI) Kidney function
CURB-65 Pneumonia severity
Wells Criteria PE or DVT probability
APACHE II ICU mortality prediction
SOFA Organ dysfunction in sepsis
💡 Tip
Chain your questions
After getting a score, keep going in the same chat: “Based on that score, should I start anticoagulation? What are the options?” Lumen will use the score in its answer.

item.title: Uploading Your Files
item.description:
Have a research paper you want summarized? A lab report you want interpreted? A protocol you want compared to current guidelines? Just attach the file to your chat and ask away. Lumen reads the document and uses it to answer your question.

How to Attach Files

1

Click the paperclip icon

In the input bar, click the paperclip icon on the left side. This opens the document tray.
2

Choose or upload a file

You can either:

item.title: Creating Documents
item.description:
Need slides for grand rounds by tomorrow? A patient education handout? A spreadsheet tracking your department’s metrics? Just tell Lumen what you need, and it creates a professional, ready-to-edit document you can download right away.

Available Tools

📄
PDF Creation
Generates formatted PDF documents — patient summaries, protocol references, research summaries, and more. Download directly from the artifact panel.
"Create a PDF summary of our discussion about this patient's treatment plan"
Documents
📊
Presentations (PPTX)
Builds PowerPoint slide decks with professional formatting. Great for grand rounds, patient education, and team presentations.
"Create a 10-slide presentation on the latest sepsis management guidelines for our grand rounds"
Documents
📋
Spreadsheets (Excel)
Generates Excel workbooks with formatted data tables, calculations, and organized sheets. Useful for tracking, reporting, and data organization.
"Create a spreadsheet tracking our department's antibiotic stewardship metrics"
Documents
🌐
Interactive HTML
Creates self-contained interactive HTML documents — calculators, decision trees, patient education materials with interactive elements.
"Build an interactive patient education page about diabetes self-management"
Interactive
🎓
CME Quiz
Generates board-style multiple-choice questions with detailed rationales. Perfect for self-study, teaching, and exam preparation.
"Create a 5-question quiz on atrial fibrillation management"
Education

Presentation Example

✦
Chat
Create a presentation on COPD management guidelines for our nursing staff education session
✦
Created presentation
title: "COPD Management Guidelines" · 8 slides

I've created an 8-slide presentation covering GOLD guidelines, pharmacotherapy stepwise approach, and acute exacerbation management.

item.title: Symptom Checker
item.description:
Describe a patient’s symptoms. The Symptom Checker runs a systematic 8-step evaluation — ESI triage, guideline review, coding, lab interpretation, and protocol recommendations.

Describe the Presentation

“45yo female, sudden onset worst headache of her life, 10/10 severity, nausea, neck stiffness. BP 180/100, HR 88. No prior headache history, no trauma.”

What Each Step Produces

1
Extract Symptoms & History
Parses the presentation into structured clinical findings.
📄
Structured Findings
Chief Complaint:Thunderclap headache, 10/10, sudden onset
Associated:Nausea, nuchal rigidity
Vitals:BP 180/100 (hypertensive), HR 88
Red flags:"Worst headache of life" + neck stiffness + HTN → SAH until proven otherwise
2
ESI Triage AssessmentTriage
🏥
Triage ResultESI Level
ESI 1 Immediate life threat
Thunderclap headache with meningismus — SAH must be emergently ruled out. Immediate CT head without contrast.
3
Clinical Guidelines
Searches for applicable clinical guidelines.
📚
Guidelines
AHA/ASA SAH Guidelines · Ottawa SAH Rule · ACR Appropriateness Criteria: Headache
4
Triage CriteriaTriage
Loads ESI severity definitions and decision rules.
5
Diagnostic CodesICD-10
🏥
ICD-10 Codes
I60.9Nontraumatic SAH, unspecified (working dx)
R51.9Headache, unspecified (if SAH ruled out)
R29.1Meningismus
6
Lab Reference RangesLab Ranges
🧪
Expected Labs
CSF xanthochromiaPositive if SAH (LP needed if CT negative)
CSF RBC countElevated in SAH, does not clear with serial tubes
7
Clinical ProtocolsChecklist
☑
SAH Workup ProtocolInteractive
Non-contrast CT head (stat)
If CT negative → LP (xanthochromia, RBC count)
If SAH confirmed → CTA for aneurysm localization
Neurosurgery consult
BP management: target SBP <160
✓
Triage Summary
Delivers the complete assessment: ESI level, key symptoms, differential, recommended workup, and disposition.
📋
Triage AssessmentFinal Report
ESI 1 · Thunderclap headache with meningismus · SAH is the primary concern · Emergent CT head → LP if negative → CTA if positive · Neurosurgery on standby · Admit to ICU if confirmed
❗
This agent is a decision support tool. The ESI triage level and recommendations support your assessment — they do not replace bedside evaluation.

item.title: Privacy & Safety
item.description:
Need to share a clinical note with a colleague but want to strip the patient’s name and MRN first? Lumen can de-identify text automatically. It also has a safe email tool for sending prior auth letters and other correspondence.

Available Tools

🔒
De-identify PHI
Automatically removes protected health information from text — names, dates, MRNs, addresses, and other identifiers. Replaces them with consistent pseudonyms so the text remains readable.
"De-identify this clinical note before I share it with the research team"
Privacy
🔓
Re-identify PHI
Restores original PHI from de-identified text using the session mapping. Only works within the same session where de-identification occurred.
"Re-identify the note from earlier"
Privacy
📧
Send Email
Sends emails on your behalf — useful for prior authorization letters, appeal correspondence, and patient communications. Only fires when you explicitly include a recipient email.
"Send this prior authorization letter to auth@insurance.com"
Communication

De-identification Example

✦
Chat
De-identify this note: "John Smith, DOB 03/15/1958, MRN 12345678, presented to the ED on 01/20/2026 with chest pain."
✦

Here's the de-identified version:

[Patient A], DOB [Date-1], MRN [ID-1], presented to the ED on [Date-2] with chest pain.
